texto: Kids grow up hearing fairy tales, but the biggest fairy tale of all, I realized at the age of four, is that life is safe. Life isn't safe, I learned. It's crazy. Evil is real. One minute you could be riding your bike on the way to get candy, and the next, you're dead. Anything could happen anywhere at any time. So now what? How was I supposed to live without giving in to the fear?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de David Kushner que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Kids grow up hearing fairy tales, but the biggest fairy tale of all, I realized at the age of fou…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, fear, safety, com foco em safety.
